title: Quararibea bracteolosa

It is a member of the order Malpighiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. Quararibea bracteolosa's classification is an important tool for understanding its relationships with other plant species and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Quararibea bracteolosa, also known as the "chuchuhuasi" tree, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The bark and wood of this tree have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including arthritis, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Quararibea bracteolosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
